skill.name: distill
skill.description: Strip designs to their essence by removing unnecessary complexity. Great design is simple, powerful, and clean.
skill.summary: A design simplification skill that helps strip away unnecessary complexity to reveal essential elements.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static scanner detected 12 false positive patterns related to cryptographic algorithm warnings and system reconnaissance. These are triggered by design terminology (strip, remove, simplify) used in context of UI/UX simplification, not actual cryptographic code or security reconnaissance. The skill is a legitimate design methodology guide with no security concerns.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner detected 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns at lines 3, 6, 11, 15, 21, 52, 115. These are FALSE POSITIVES triggered by design terminology (strip, remove, simplify, essential) that appear in crypto-breaking contexts but are actually about design simplification. The skill teaches UI/UX simplification techniques, not cryptographic methods.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All detected lines contain legitimate design terminology in context of UI/UX simplification - words like 'strip', 'remove', 'simplify' describe design patterns, not cryptographic algorithms. No cryptographic code exists in the file.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner detected 'system reconnaissance' at line 61. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The actual text is 'Remove redundancy: If it's said elsewhere, don't repeat it here' which discusses removing redundant UI copy, not system reconnaissance.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The phrase 'If it's said elsewhere, don't repeat it here' is standard UX writing advice about avoiding redundant content, not security reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Simplify and Strip Design to Its Essence
content.value_statement: This skill helps AI assistants simplify complex designs by removing unnecessary elements, creating clean and focused user interfaces that guide users to their goals.
actual_capabilities: Analyze designs to identify sources of complexity and visual clutter
Systematically simplify information architecture and visual hierarchy
Guide users through ruthless simplification while preserving essential functionality
Reduce color palettes, typography variations, and decorative elements
Simplify layouts, interactions, and content for better usability
Verify that simplification improves usability without removing necessary features
limitations: Does not modify code directly - provides guidance for human implementation
Requires design context from frontend-design skill to operate effectively
Cannot evaluate complex domain-specific requirements that need business logic
May not handle accessibility requirements without explicit context

use_cases.title: UI Design Cleanup
use_cases.description: A UX designer has a cluttered dashboard with too many buttons, colors, and information competing for attention. The skill helps identify what's essential and creates a simplification plan.

use_cases.title: Landing Page Streamlining
use_cases.description: A marketing team wants to reduce their landing page to its core message. The skill guides removal of unnecessary elements while preserving conversion paths.

use_cases.title: Design System Refinement
use_cases.description: A design system has accumulated too many components and variants. The skill helps consolidate and reduce to essential patterns that cover 90% of use cases.
use_cases.target_user: Design system maintainers and UI architects

prompt_templates.title: Basic Simplification Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the distill skill to simplify the design of [component or feature]. Focus on removing visual clutter and identifying what's essential.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to simplify a specific UI component or page

prompt_templates.title: Full Design Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the distill skill to analyze the entire design system. Identify all sources of complexity and create a plan for systematic simplification across information architecture, visual design, layout, interactions, and content.
prompt_templates.scenario: When conducting a comprehensive design review

prompt_templates.title: Component Consolidation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the distill skill to review our component library. Identify components with too many variants and recommend consolidation to essential variations that cover most use cases.
prompt_templates.scenario: When optimizing a design system or component library

prompt_templates.title: Performance Through Simplicity
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the distill skill to simplify the design with a focus on improving performance. Recommend what can be removed or deferred to reduce initial load time.
prompt_templates.scenario: When performance is a key concern and simpler design can help

output_examples.input: Simplify this checkout flow that has 5 steps, 12 form fields, and multiple payment options visible at once
output_examples.output: Recommendations include: Consolidate to 2-step flow (shipping + payment), auto-fill address from email, show only primary payment method initially, hide advanced options behind 'More options', reduce form fields to 5 essential ones, use progressive disclosure for optional fields

output_examples.input: Our dashboard has 8 different charts, 4 sidebar navigation sections, and a header with 6 action buttons
output_examples.output: Recommendations include: Reduce to 2-3 key charts on main view, move others to tabbed views, consolidate sidebar to 2 sections (main nav + settings), reduce header to 1 primary action + icon menu, use white space instead of card containers

best_practices: Always run the Context Gathering Protocol from frontend-design skill before starting simplification
Focus on the ONE primary user goal and remove everything that distracts from it
Use progressive disclosure to hide complexity rather than removing features entirely
Verify that simplification improves task completion time and reduces cognitive load
anti_patterns: Removing necessary functionality just to simplify - simplicity is not feature-less
Oversimplifying complex domains where the complexity matches the actual task difficulty
Removing information users need to make informed decisions
Eliminating hierarchy completely so nothing stands out as important

faq.question: What is the distill skill?
faq.answer: The distill skill helps AI assistants guide users through simplifying designs. It provides a systematic approach to removing unnecessary complexity while preserving essential functionality and usability.

faq.question: Do I need any other skills to use distill?
faq.answer: Yes, you should use the frontend-design skill first to gather design context. If no design context exists, run the teach-impeccable skill to establish the baseline.

faq.question: Can distill modify code directly?
faq.answer: No, distill provides guidance and recommendations. It does not directly modify code or design files. Users must implement the suggestions themselves.

faq.question: What does simplification mean in this context?
faq.answer: Simplification means removing visual clutter, reducing choices, consolidating similar elements, and creating clear hierarchy. It is not about removing features but about removing obstacles between users and their goals.

faq.question: How does distill handle accessibility?
faq.answer: Distill explicitly states that you should never sacrifice accessibility for simplicity. Clear labels and ARIA attributes are still required. The skill helps simplify without compromising accessibility.

faq.question: What is the paradox of choice mentioned in the skill?
faq.answer: The paradox of choice is a psychological phenomenon where offering too many options leads to decision paralysis and user dissatisfaction. Distill addresses this by reducing choices to clear, essential options.
